data.title: The Elite Competitor
data.body:
**Profile:** Driven by PRs, race times, and podium finishes.

**Daily Routine:**
- 5:00 AM - Wake up, hydrate, light mobility
- 5:30 AM - Morning run (10-15 miles, structured workout)
- 7:30 AM - Post-run strength training
- 8:30 AM - Protein-rich breakfast, ice bath
- 12:00 PM - Nap and recovery
- 4:00 PM - Second run (easy 5-6 miles)
- 6:00 PM - Stretching, foam rolling
- 9:00 PM - Early bed for optimal recovery

**Mindset:** Every run has a purpose. Data-driven training.

data.title: The Morning Jogger
data.body:
**Profile:** Runs for health, stress relief, and to start the day right. Not chasing times.

**Daily Routine:**
- 6:00 AM - Wake up, coffee
- 6:30 AM - Easy 3-5 mile jog around neighborhood
- 7:15 AM - Shower and breakfast
- 7:45 AM - Off to work
- Evening - Light stretching or yoga
- 10:00 PM - Bed

**Mindset:** Consistency over intensity. Running is meditation.

data.title: The Ultra Endurance Runner
data.body:
**Profile:** Lives for 50Ks, 100-milers, and multi-day adventures. Pain is temporary.

**Daily Routine:**
- 4:30 AM - Wake up, mental preparation
- 5:00 AM - Long run (15-30 miles on weekends)
- Weekdays: 8-12 mile morning runs
- Throughout day - Constant fueling (300-500 cal/hr during long runs)
- Afternoon - Cross-training (hiking, cycling)
- Evening - Gear prep, nutrition planning
- 8:30 PM - Early sleep (8-9 hours essential)

**Mindset:** Embrace suffering. The mind quits before the body.

data.title: The Social Runner
data.body:
**Profile:** Running is about community, friendships, and shared experiences.

**Daily Routine:**
- 6:00 AM - Check group chat for meet-up plans
- 6:30 AM - Meet running club at park
- 7:00 AM - Group run (pace varies, conversation flows)
- 8:00 AM - Post-run coffee with crew
- Saturday - Parkrun or local 5K events
- Evening - Online running community engagement

**Mindset:** Miles are better with friends. Every run is a social event.

data.title: The Trail Adventurer
data.body:
**Profile:** Escapes to mountains, forests, and dirt paths. Road running feels like prison.

**Daily Routine:**
- 5:30 AM - Drive to trailhead
- 6:00 AM - Trail run with elevation (8-15 miles)
- 9:00 AM - Nature photography, wildlife spotting
- Afternoon - Gear maintenance, trail research
- Evening - Plan next adventure, study maps
- Weekends - All-day mountain expeditions

**Mindset:** The journey matters more than the pace. Nature heals.

data.title: The Speed Demon
data.body:
**Profile:** Track intervals, tempo runs, and explosive power. Born to sprint.

**Daily Routine:**
- 6:00 AM - Dynamic warm-up (20 min)
- 6:30 AM - Track workout (400m repeats, 800m intervals)
- 7:30 AM - Plyometrics and sprint drills
- 8:00 AM - High-protein breakfast
- Afternoon - Weight room (explosive lifts)
- Evening - Video analysis of form
- Rest days - Active recovery only

**Mindset:** Speed is a skill. Every millisecond counts.

data.title: The Beginner (Couch to 5K)
data.body:
**Profile:** Just started the journey. Every run is a victory.

**Daily Routine:**
- 7:00 AM - Wake up, motivational podcast
- 7:30 AM - Walk/run intervals (C25K app)
- 8:00 AM - Celebrate completing the workout
- Throughout day - Research running tips
- Evening - Lay out clothes for tomorrow
- 3x per week - Running days
- Off days - Walking, rest

**Mindset:** Progress over perfection. Showing up is winning.

data.title: Core Running Fundamentals
data.body:
**Universal principles that connect all runner types:**

- **Consistency** - Regular running beats sporadic heroics
- **Recovery** - Growth happens during rest
- **Nutrition** - Fuel determines performance
- **Sleep** - The ultimate performance enhancer
- **Progression** - Gradual increases prevent injury
- **Listen to body** - Pain vs discomfort awareness
- **Gear matters** - Right shoes prevent 80% of injuries
